mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, or 5d return volatility drops below 1.2% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust economic demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST rise by > 1.5% quarter-over-quarter, or pairwise correlation < 0.3 between proxies.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4%) drives wage inflation, prompting monetary policy restraint and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4%, or wage growth (PAYEMS) exceeds 200k without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 100) indicates weak supply chain capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 100, or 5d return volatility exceeds 2.5% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 690 billion) reflects strong demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in > 45% of instances where RSAFS exceeds 690 billion, or RSAFS growth correlates negatively with SPX returns (r < -0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or employment growth decouples from equity performance (r < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO < 100) and housing starts (HOUST < 1350k) indicate supply-side weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both INDPRO and HOUST fall below thresholds, or pairwise correlation between proxies exceeds 0.75.
